timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A man gives a widow his business card disguised as a condolence gesture, hinting at legal assistance, after receiving money from another attendee at a funeral.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Frank Galvin is introduced to a widow by a funeral director and hands her his business card under the pretense of offering help, shortly after receiving a cash referral fee.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: acquire clients through funeral referrals
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: actively soliciting legal clients at a funeral through personal introductions and emotional appeals
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: ethical compromise in client acquisition
evidence_quotes: The Second Man reaches behind the First Man's back and puts a discreetly folded ten-dollar bill into his hands.
FUNERAL DIRECTOR Mrs. Dee, this is Frank Galvin -- a very good friend of ours, and a very fine attorney.
GALVIN removes a business card from his jacket pocket and hands it to her as if he were giving her money.
GALVIN (thoughtfully realizes he is usurping her time) Well... He shakes her hand and moves on.

relation_updates: establishes transactional relationship with funeral director

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The Funeral Manager confronts Galvin, forbidding him from returning, and shoves him aside while mourners arrive for the funeral.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: The funeral manager explicitly bars Galvin from returning and physically shoves him aside, ending his access to funeral homes and marking his exclusion from professional engagement with grieving families.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: professionally ostracized and emotionally defeated
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how Galvin will rebuild after losing access to clients and public dignity
evidence_quotes: I don't want you coming back here. Ever. Do you understand?
Those are bereaved people in there.
The Funeral Manager gives Galvin a small shove,
Standing watching the mourners enter.

relation_updates: formal expulsion from funeral access by manager

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin ignores a persistent caller, tears up files, drinks heavily, and stares at his empty wall, indifferent to the voice pleading from the fallen phone.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Galvin discards legal files and academic degrees into a wastebasket while drinking heavily, showing a physical rejection of his professional life.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: abandoning his legal career
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: fully disengaged from legal work
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: no clear path forward, only numb repetition
evidence_quotes: He takes out an armload of files, carries them to a wastebasket and throws them in.
He repeats softly to himself, as a litany, "It doesn't make a bit of difference, it doesn't make a bit of difference..."
Galvin pays no attention. Drinks his drink and gazes at the wall -- now empty of degrees.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin photographs a frail, tube-covered patient, replaces her chart, places his belongings on the bed, and sits silently smoking while gazing at her.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Galvin takes photographs of the comatose Deborah Ann Kaye and sits silently by her bed, showing a visible shift in his demeanor from procedural documentation to personal engagement.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: documenting evidence and observing the victim's condition
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: personally affected and emotionally present at the scene
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to prove medical negligence
evidence_quotes: Galvin stops at the foot of one bed and reads the chart.
Takes another one. Puts down camera, sits on the end of the bed gazing at the unseen form.
He lights a cigarette, and sits looking at her.

relation_updates: develops personal connection to Deborah Ann Kaye
auxiliary.persona_anchor: moral awakening through direct patient contact

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin rejects the Bishop's $210,000 settlement, unable to accept money that feels like a bribe, despite his struggling law practice and only one client.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Galvin rejects the $210,000 settlement offer from the hospital, recognizing that accepting it would be complicit in covering up medical negligence.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to seek justice for Deborah Ann Kaye rather than personal financial gain
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Galvin commits to taking the case to trial despite his failing practice and lack of resources.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how he will successfully litigate the case with limited experience and financial means
evidence_quotes: It's not too good. I've only got one client.
We have been paid to look the other way.
That was our insurance company's recommendation.

relation_updates: adversarial stance toward Bishop Brophy and the hospital
moral alignment with his client, Deborah Ann Kaye
status_updates: commits to pursuing the case in court
rejects easy resolution despite financial desperation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin and Mickey continue reviewing cases, discussing Mary Rooney's potential testimony and recalling past cases, with Galvin showing strong recall and referencing his mentor.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Galvin reviews case details with Mickey, recalls key medical and legal points, identifies missing nurse deposition, and references prior cases to strengthen strategy.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: building a strong case strategy
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: actively preparing for trial with renewed focus and direction
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: lack of deposition from obstetrical nurse Mary Rooney
evidence_quotes: We've got her sister. Testifies she had a meal one hour before she was admitted to the hospital.
Admittance form is wrong.

relation_updates: reinforces collaborative dynamic with Mickey

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin checks mailboxes, uses a letter opener to open Rooney's box, and looks through her letters, including a phone bill.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Galvin opens Mary Rooney's mailbox with a letter opener and searches her mail, including her phone bill, to locate her.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking evidence through unauthorized access to personal information
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: begins active investigation by physically intruding into a witness's private affairs
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: moral justification for breaking rules to pursue justice
evidence_quotes: takes heavy letter opener from his jacket pocket and pries open the Rooney mailbox.
He extracts letters and rifles through them.
ANGLE - P.O.V. Mary Rooney's phone bill.

relation_updates: intrusive and adversarial relationship with Mary Rooney
auxiliary.persona_anchor: driven, willing to bend rules for truth

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin, posing as a subscription agent, contacts Kathy Price, who corrects his details and ends the call. He notes her name and workplace, Chelsea Childcare, after failed renewal pitch.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Galvin pretends to be a subscription agent to obtain identifying details from Kathy Price, who corrects his false premises and ends the call, but not before revealing her name and workplace.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: obtaining leads by infiltrating through false identity
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: successfully acquires the name Kathy Price and her employer, Chelsea Childcare, through a brief deceptive phone call
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: use of deception to obtain information raises ethical concerns
evidence_quotes: Galvin introduces himself cautiously, claiming to call from Professional Nurse Quarterly.
The woman corrects him—her name is Kathy Price.
she corrects him: it’s Chelsea Childcare, not the Manhattan Health Center.
Galvin scribbles on a notepad as the line goes dead: 'Kathy Price. Chelsea Childcare.'

relation_updates: adversarial and interrupted interaction with Kathy Price
auxiliary.persona_anchor: persistent and resourceful, willing to use subterfuge for investigative gain

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Judge disallows Kathy Costello's testimony and Xerox form as hearsay, orders jury to disregard her entire testimony, citing lack of original evidence and improper rebuttal witness use.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Galvin's key witness testimony and supporting document are disallowed by the judge; he formally objects and notes an exception, then begins writing, indicating a shift toward preserving the record and regrouping.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: preserve the record for appeal and find another way to win
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Galvin's case collapses in real time, but he remains engaged and begins documenting his response
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how Galvin will proceed after losing his primary evidence and witness
evidence_quotes: GALVIN I object, your Honor...
JUDGE Overruled...
GALVIN Exception!
Galvin takes a sheet of legal paper and starts writing on it.

relation_updates: loses judicial support temporarily

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The jury considers awarding more than the plaintiff requested, sparking hope in Galvin and Mickey, while the judge instructs them to retire for further deliberation amid courtroom tension.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Galvin shifts from resignation to cautious hope when the jury asks if they can award more than the plaintiff requested, suggesting they may rule strongly in his favor.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: secure a favorable jury verdict
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: moves from defeated resignation to tentative optimism
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: what the final verdict will be
evidence_quotes: Galvin, who appears utterly defeated, nodding his head in quiet resignation
Mickey begins to smile

relation_updates: experiences shared emotional support with Mickey

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin, affected by Laura's note, silently rejects reconciliation, shares a moment with Mickey, and leaves, pausing briefly with lingering longing before closing the door on the past.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Galvin silently turns away from Laura, who holds a note expressing hope for reconciliation, and instead shares a quiet moment with Mickey before leaving together.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: committed to a new path of personal and professional integrity
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: chooses solidarity with Mickey and moves forward, showing emotional separation from his past relationship
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: lingering emotional attachment to Laura
evidence_quotes: From his point of view, Laura appears, standing hesitantly at the far end, looking lost and silently pleading.
He stares for a long moment, the weight of the moment pressing in.
Without a word, Galvin turns his back on her.
Galvin hesitates as Mickey steps through. He looks back, longing in his eyes.

relation_updates: distances himself from Laura
affirms bond with Mickey

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Frank Galvin begins as a discredited ambulance chaser, emotionally and professionally broken, surviving on funeral referrals and alcohol. After being publicly humiliated and cut off from his old network, he hits rock bottom, discarding his legal identity. A medical malpractice case reignites his purpose, and through direct contact with the comatose victim, he shifts from self-interest to moral commitment. Despite pressure to settle, he refuses a bribe, embraces trial, and confronts systemic corruption. After setbacks and personal collapse, he uncovers a cover-up, regains momentum, and ultimately chooses integrity over comfort, walking away from his past to embrace a renewed sense of justice.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin, a lawyer, flirts with Laura at a bar, challenges her to a bet about her divorce, learns her name, and invites her to dinner, which she hesitantly follows.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Laura reveals her name and agrees to dinner with Galvin after deflecting personal questions, marking the beginning of direct engagement between them.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: engaging with Galvin despite initial hesitation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: no longer anonymous to Galvin and has agreed to a dinner date with him
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: uncertainty about emotional availability post-divorce
evidence_quotes: LAURA: Still looking.
LAURA: I changed my room at the Hotel.
LAURA: The TV didn't work.
What's your name?

relation_updates: begins romantic involvement with Galvin

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin, preparing for jury selection, reflects on the jury's cynicism and hope for justice, expressing his intent to try to do something right in the case.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Laura listens as Galvin expresses his hope to do something right in the case, reflecting his internal struggle and desire for moral integrity.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: understanding Galvin's inner struggle
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally invested in Galvin's determination to pursue justice
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Galvin will follow through on his intention to do something right
evidence_quotes: Maybe what?
Maybe I can do something right.
And is that what you're going to do? (a beat) Is that what you're going to do...?
That's what I'm going to try to do.

relation_updates: gains insight into Galvin's professional and moral conflict

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin and Laura, both unsteady, enter a dark bedroom; he turns on the light, notices his wife's photo, begins to turn it over, but Laura stops him and starts undressing.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Laura stops Galvin from hiding his wife's photo and initiates physical intimacy by beginning to undress.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: physically intimate with Galvin, acknowledging his past relationship
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how Galvin's past will affect their relationship
evidence_quotes: LAURA That's all right.
She starts taking off her blouse.

relation_updates: accepts Galvin's unresolved past
status_updates: enters into physical relationship with Galvin

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Laura, in Galvin's bathrobe, sits by his worktable reading; she pauses, looks toward the closed bedroom door, and reflects quietly.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Laura stays with Galvin after he becomes distressed about a setback in his case, encourages him to rest, and remains in his apartment overnight.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: support Galvin through stress
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Laura is present in Galvin's private space overnight, observing and waiting while he rests.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Galvin's ability to cope under pressure
evidence_quotes: LAURA Why don't you get some rest?
LAURA You can't work if you can't think. You get in bed. It's all right. I'll stay here with you. It's all right. Come on...
She leads him into the bedroom.

relation_updates: increased emotional closeness with Galvin
assumes caretaker role
status_updates: present in Galvin's private space overnight

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin collapses under pressure, isolates in the bathroom, and struggles to breathe; Laura confronts him, asserting he's self-sabotaging, and urges letting go as he resists her insight.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Laura confronts Galvin about his defeatist attitude and self-blame, rejecting his passivity and insisting he take responsibility.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: push Galvin to face responsibility
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: refuses to comfort Galvin's helplessness, insists he stop avoiding the trial and grow up
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Galvin can overcome his self-doubt and engage in the trial
evidence_quotes: You're talking like a drunk.
What is your wife's picture doing by the side of your...
We've all got to let go.

relation_updates: shifts from caretaker to challenger
asserts emotional boundaries

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Concannon welcomes Laura back to law practice, handing her a confidential document from their firm, signaling her return to the legal world after personal changes.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Laura is formally welcomed back to the firm by Concannon, who hands her a confidential document, marking her return to legal practice.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: returning to law practice
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: officially back in the legal world, receiving a confidential firm document
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: personal cost of returning to a high-pressure legal career
evidence_quotes: You finished your marriage. You wanted to come back and practice law. You wanted to come back to the world.
He hands the red-backed document to her.
stamped CONCANNON, BARKER, WHITE. Confidential. Eyes only.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Laura finds a handwritten note from Joe in a Hotel Lincoln envelope, reading it in dim light before sitting down, contemplating his message about leaving together after 'this is over.'
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Laura discovers and reads Joe's handwritten note proposing they leave together after everything ends.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: contemplating life beyond the current crisis
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: sitting in silence after reading Joe's note, visibly affected and uncertain about the future
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether 'when this is over' will ever come and what 'going away' really means

A hotel envelope, The Hotel Lincoln - Boston, Mass. on the letterhead.
Laura's hands open the message, take out a sheet of yellow legal paper.
The legal sheet. It reads, handwritten: Laura. I'm going to try. When this is over can we go away? Joe.

relation_updates: renewed emotional connection with Joe

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin and Mickey make false calls as Laura grows restless, seeking cigarettes; she leaves, pausing to see Galvin still on the phone, who briefly smiles at her.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Laura attempts to speak with Galvin about a personal matter, is rebuffed, and departs after a brief, wordless exchange in which he offers her a half-smile.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin knocks Laura to the floor, upsets the table; a bystander intervenes as she repeatedly says 'it's all right,' appearing in shock while he struggles to speak.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Laura evolves from a recently divorced woman tentatively entering a new relationship with Frank Galvin to an emotionally invested partner who supports him through professional and personal crises. As their bond deepens, she confronts his self-destructive tendencies and reengages with her legal career, only to face betrayal and violence when Galvin discovers a confidential report she unknowingly carries about him. The relationship collapses, leaving her emotional future unresolved as Galvin silently rejects reconciliation after the trial.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mickey confronts a drunk Galvin, slaps him, and ends their partnership, urging him to handle a moneymaker case. Galvin, left alone, sits with a cigarette, his shaking hand revealing a wedding ring and rosary.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: mentor and former partner confronting decline
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mickey ends his professional partnership with Galvin after confronting his alcoholism and failure to prepare for a case, refusing to continue enabling him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to stop supporting Galvin's self-destructive path
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: no longer professionally involved with Galvin, having severed ties and left him to manage his own responsibilities
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Galvin can recover without Mickey's support

This is it. I got you a good case, it's a moneymaker. You do it right and it will take care of you. But I'm through.
Life is too short, and I'm too old.
Mickey walks out of the office. Slams the door.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin interrupts Mickey Morrissey in the Lawyers Room at the Courthouse, insisting they leave to talk over a drink, prompting Mickey to follow reluctantly.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin and Mickey continue reviewing cases, discussing Mary Rooney's potential testimony and recalling past cases, with Galvin showing strong recall and referencing his mentor.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Frank, after exposing corruption, is indicted for jury tampering, jailed, and disbarred; charges are dropped after he recants, but he loses his job, marriage, and turns to drinking for years.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mickey recounts Frank Galvin's breakdown after discovering his law firm's corruption, emphasizing Frank's emotional reaction and subsequent downfall.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to explain how Frank's idealism led to his professional and personal collapse
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Mickey portrays himself as a pragmatic voice who warned Frank against moral absolutism
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Frank will again risk his stability by pursuing justice
evidence_quotes: He comes to me in tears. He thinks that anybody who knows what a 'spinnaker' is got to be a saint.
I told him 'Franky, wake up. These people are sharks. What do you think they got so rich from? Doing good?'
You like that story?

relation_updates: reinforces Mickey's role as Frank's longtime observer and critic
status_updates: establishes Mickey's perspective on legal corruption and personal failure
auxiliary.persona_anchor: cynical witness to past idealism's cost

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin contacts Dr. Thompson, a potential witness in the Deborah Ann Kaye case, after reconsidering his strategy; Mickey reacts with frustration to Galvin's renewed efforts.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mickey throws Galvin's box of Macanudo cigars across the room in disgust upon seeing Galvin revert to self-destructive patterns and renege on the case.

timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Mickey can sustain influence over Galvin's choices

relation_updates: shows Mickey's emotional investment in Galvin's recovery
status_updates: Mickey takes an active stance against Galvin's relapse

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dr. Thompson affirms the patient's good care as Mickey shifts tone, questioning the impact on the girl's life and referencing 'Code Blue,' signaling concern to Galvin.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mickey shifts from aggressive questioning of Dr. Thompson to expressing doubt about the case's value, then uses a 'Code Blue' reference and a subtle shrug to communicate concern to Galvin.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to assess the witness's credibility and convey reservations about the case nonverbally
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Mickey appears skeptical of the case's merits and communicates this doubt to Galvin through tone and gesture
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether pursuing the case is justified if it damages reputations without improving the patient's life
evidence_quotes: Then what good would it do to ruin the reputation of two men, to help a girl whose life's not going to be changed in the least?

Mickey half-turns to Galvin, shrugs minutely, meaning, \"We're in trouble.\"

relation_updates: demonstrates tacit understanding and trust with Galvin through nonverbal cues
status_updates: Mickey transitions from confrontation to strategic assessment
auxiliary.persona_anchor: strategic assessor using tone shifts and nonverbal cues

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dr. Towler explains that Deborah Kaye's anemia made her brain more vulnerable, potentially causing damage in just two minutes without oxygen, undermining Galvin's argument.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: moral and professional barometer for Galvin
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mickey shakes his head sadly at Galvin after Galvin's line of questioning inadvertently strengthens the defense's position.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Galvin realizes he has made a critical error in questioning, prompted by Mickey's visible reaction.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the case is weakening due to Galvin's strategic misstep in court
evidence_quotes: Galvin is struck dumb. He has just made a terrible error. He looks at Mickey.
Mickey looks at Galvin. He shakes his head sadly.

relation_updates: expresses nonverbal disapproval, showing investment in Galvin's performance
status_updates: reacts to trial developments with emotional engagement
auxiliary.persona_anchor: nonverbal critic and emotional feedback mechanism

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin confronts Mickey, rejecting defeat; insists this case is everything and demands Mickey choose whether to stay involved or leave.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Galvin pressures Mickey to reaffirm his involvement after Mickey declares the case lost.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Mickey must decide whether to stay in a failing case
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Mickey remains in the case despite expressing that it is unwinnable
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Mickey's motivation to continue is unclear
evidence_quotes: I think we tried that. The case is over.
It's over. Period. There'll be no other cases...
Galvin turns on him, animal-like.

relation_updates: Galvin asserts dominance in the partnership
Mickey resists but remains present

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mickey finds a confidential report in Laura's purse about Joseph Galvin while searching for cigarettes, hides it when she finishes her call, and remains silent about the discovery.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mickey discovers a confidential report on Joseph Galvin in Laura's purse while searching for cigarettes and chooses not to reveal his knowledge after putting it back.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to obtain cigarettes
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Mickey becomes aware of Laura's involvement in a confidential investigation of Joseph Galvin but remains silent and conceals his discovery.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Mickey will disclose what he saw or take action based on the information
evidence_quotes: He finds a pack of cigarettes but stops abruptly when he sees something else: a red-backed legal form.
He pulls it out, flips to a page titled 'Report on Joseph Galvin,' filled with notes on haunts, habits, scribbled in multiple inks.
Mickey quietly replaces the form and cigarettes, closes the purse, and turns to face her.

relation_updates: grows suspicious of Laura's loyalty
becomes aware of Laura's connection to Galvin's adversaries
status_updates: enters a position of silent complicity

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Galvin, affected by Laura's note, silently rejects reconciliation, shares a moment with Mickey, and leaves, pausing briefly with lingering longing before closing the door on the past.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mickey stands by Galvin as he silently rejects Laura's attempt at reconciliation, then shares a quiet moment of solidarity before they leave the courthouse together.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: leaving the courthouse together after the trial, facing an uncertain future
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: outcome of the jury deliberation and what comes next for Galvin and Mickey
evidence_quotes: Mickey lights Galvin's cigarette, his hand trembling noticeably.
Mickey looks at Laura too, then back at Galvin.
Mickey follows suit.
Together, they push through the exit door, turning up their collars against the cold.

relation_updates: mutual solidarity with Galvin after shared rejection of Laura
reciprocated physical gesture of support
status_updates: exits the courthouse, marking the end of the trial phase
auxiliary.persona_anchor: enduring ally in emotional and professional aftermath

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Mickey evolves from a detached former partner to a reluctant but pivotal ally in Galvin's moral and professional resurgence. Initially cutting ties due to Galvin's self-destruction, he gradually re-engages, shifting from pragmatic resistance to active support in both legal and personal matters. His role expands beyond legal counsel to emotional barometer and confidant, culminating in silent complicity and shared resolve through the trial's uncertain end. Despite skepticism and strategic disagreements, Mickey remains a grounding presence, reflecting the tension between legal realism and hard-won idealism.
